subject: BUG: Timezone/Zulu issue causing duplicates
body:
Harvey,

## Problem

Found 14+ duplicate events in PROD caused by timezone handling differences between sources. Events appear with different UTC times but are the same local event.

## Examples

### 1. CONEXION Miami (Festival)

| Source | Start (UTC) | Start (Miami EDT) |
|--------|-------------|-------------------|
| porter (harvester) | 2026-04-10T01:30Z | Apr 9, 9:30 PM |
| porter-booker | 2026-04-09T21:30Z | Apr 9, 5:30 PM |

Both should be April 9. Description says "April 9-12". The harvester version appears to store local time as if it were UTC.

### 2. Traditional Milonga Buenas Noches (Orlando)

| Source | Start (UTC) | Start (Orlando EDT) |
|--------|-------------|---------------------|
| porter (harvester) | 2026-04-11T03:45Z | Apr 10, 11:45 PM |
| porter-booker | 2026-04-10T23:45Z | Apr 10, 7:45 PM |

Same issue - 4 hour offset suggesting local-as-UTC.

### 3. More duplicates found

- Milonga La Mariposa (Minneapolis)
- Mala Leche (New York)
- Milonga TRASNOCHANDO (Somerville)
- International Cuisine Fiesta (Richmond)
- SCILLA (Roma)
- Dancer's Musicality (Albuquerque)
- Milonga Arrabal (Los Angeles)
- TSoM Milonga (Minneapolis)
- Volver with Ariadna & Fernando (New York)

## Root Cause

Likely one of:
1. Source API returns local time without timezone indicator
2. Parser treats local time as UTC
3. Missing timezone conversion before storing

## Porter's Fix

Updated dedup logic to:
- Match on title + city + DATE (ignore time)
- Merge descriptions when duplicate found

This prevents future duplicates but doesn't fix the source.

## Request

1. Check how tangoevents-au (and other sources) handle timezones
2. Ensure dates are stored in actual UTC, not local-as-UTC
3. Consider adding timezone field to source data

## Impact

Users see duplicate events. I've cleaned up CONEXION but 13+ others remain.

— Porter
